title: PureHealth named top UAE healthcare brand portfolio as value rises to $3bn - Arabian Business: Latest News
summary: PureHealth retains position as UAE’s leading healthcare brand portfolio, with brand value rising 23 per cent to AED11bn ($3bn)
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Skip to content

PureHealth has retained its position as the UAE’s leading healthcare brand portfolio in Brand Finance’s UAE 50 and Middle East 150 2026 rankings, with its combined brand value rising 23 per cent year-on-year to AED11bn ($3bn).

The rankings also highlighted the performance of the group’s subsidiaries, with Daman, SEHA and Sheikh Shakhbout Medical City (SSMC) all recognised among the UAE’s 10 strongest brands across all sectors.

The latest results reinforce PureHealth’s expanding presence in the healthcare sector as the group continues to scale internationally and strengthen its integrated care and insurance platform.

According to the rankings, Daman ranked seventh among the UAE’s strongest brands with a Brand Strength Index (BSI) score of 83.3.

Its brand value increased 19 per cent year-on-year to AED1.2bn ($326.7m).

PureHealth in the UAE

SEHA ranked ninth among the UAE’s strongest brands with a BSI score of 80.4 and achieved a brand value of AED3.61bn ($982.8m), representing growth of 21 per cent from the previous year.

Sheikh Shakhbout Medical City (SSMC) ranked 10th among the UAE’s strongest brands with a BSI score of 80.3.

SSMC recorded a brand value of AED1.2bn ($326.7m), marking an increase of 50 per cent from 2025.

Shaista Asif, Group CEO of PureHealth, said the rankings reflected the strength of the group’s integrated healthcare platform and its role in shaping the future of healthcare in the UAE.

She said: “By bringing together care delivery, insurance and advanced clinical capabilities, we are building a more connected, data-driven system designed to deliver better outcomes at every stage of the consumer journey. As we continue to expand internationally, we remain focused on scaling this model and accelerating innovation in in AI-enabled care and consumer solutions”.

The rankings build on PureHealth’s sustained growth trajectory and expanding international footprint, as the group continues to scale its presence across key global markets and strengthen its integrated Care and Cover model.

Brand Finance’s UAE 50 – 2026 report identified economic diversification and AI adoption as key drivers of brand growth.
